claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start by finding out about the child you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

This helps determine which eligibility requirements apply to your situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm looking to claim for my son Alex. He's 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 17 years old, he falls into the 16-19 age category. For children in this age group, there are some additional requirements around education or training that we need to check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
For Alex to qualify for Child Benefit at age 17, we need to know about his current situation with education or training.

**Which of the following best describes Alex's situation?**

1. **Currently in education or training** - He's enrolled in a course or approved training programme
2. **Recently left education or training** - He's left within the last few months
3. **Neither** - He's not in education/training and hasn't been recently

Which option applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Neither really - he left school and he's not doing any courses or training at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex is 17, has left school, and is not currently in any education or training, I need to check one more thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. There is a **20-week extension** that could apply to Alex if he's recently left education, but certain conditions need to be met.

**To qualify for the extension, Alex would need to have:**

1. Left approved education or training **within the last 20 weeks**, AND
2. Registered with one of these qualifying services:
   - A government careers service
   - Connexions
   - Armed services

**Can I ask:**
- **When did Alex leave school/education?** (approximately)
- **Has he registered with any careers service or similar support?**

If he left more than 20 weeks ago, or hasn't registered with a qualifying service, he unfortunately wouldn't qualify for the extension.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any careers service or anything like that. He's just been at home trying to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to give you an outcome for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Result for Alex

**Result: Not Eligible** ❌

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, you are **not currently eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why Alex doesn't qualify:

For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can only be claimed if they are:
1. **In approved full-time education or training** (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study), OR
2. **Recently left education** (within the last 20 weeks) **AND** registered with a qualifying service such as a government careers service, Connexions, or the armed services

Since Alex has left school, isn't in any education or training, and hasn't registered with a careers service, he doesn't meet either of these requirements.

---

### What you could do next:

There are a couple of options that could change Alex's eligibility:

1. **Register with a careers service** - If Alex left education within the last 20 weeks, he could register with a government careers service (like the National Careers Service in England). This would provide a **20-week extension** of Child Benefit eligibility while he decides his next steps. However, this extension ends after 20 weeks or when he turns 18, whichever comes first.

2. **Return to approved education or training** - If Alex starts an approved course (such as A-levels, BTECs, T-levels, or NVQs at Level 1-3) with at least 12 hours of supervised study per week, you could claim Child Benefit for him again until he turns 20.

For more information, you can visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which places him in the 16-19 age category. For children in this age group, Child Benefit can only continue if: (1) They are in approved full-time education or training (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study at a qualifying level), OR (2) They have left education within the last 20 weeks AND have registered with a qualifying service (government careers service, Connexions, or armed services). Alex has left school, is not currently in any education or training, and has not registered with any careers service or qualifying support programme. Therefore, he does not meet the criteria for the 20-week extension, and Child Benefit cannot be claimed for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is seeking Child Benefit for one child, Alex (age 17). Since Alex has left education and has not registered with a qualifying careers service, he does not meet the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.question: Has the child (aged 16-17) left education within the last 20 weeks AND registered with a qualifying service?
response.help_text: 20-week extension available if child has left approved education/training and registered with: government careers service, Connexions, or armed services. Extension ends at week 20 or when child turns 18, whichever is sooner.

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.reason: 20-week extension not applicable (child not registered with qualifying service, extension period expired, or child turned 18)
response.guidance: The 20-week extension for 16-17 year olds who leave education is only available if they register with a government careers service, Connexions service, or armed services. The extension ends after 20 weeks or when the child turns 18, whichever is sooner. If extension has expired and child is not in approved education/training, Child Benefit stops.
